steps.title: Use the predictor finding
steps.content: The passage first presents the expectation that flowers with high sugar concentrations should attract hummingbirds. The study then overturns that expectation: visit frequencies were not influenced by nectar traits. Instead, physical compatibility between flower corollas and hummingbird bills more strongly predicted interaction patterns.

steps.title: Avoid overstating nectar effects
steps.content: Choice A overstates the finding with “consistently” and says hummingbirds prefer low nectar rewards, which the text never claims. Choice C invents nectar qualities as a secondary factor, even though the study says visit frequencies were not influenced by nectar traits. Choice D invents uncertainty about which plants produce high-reward nectar. Choice B cautiously follows from the finding that compatibility may lead hummingbirds to visit lower-reward flowers over higher-reward ones. The answer is B.
